title: Allionia arenaria
taxonomy: Allionia arenaria is a species classified in the family Nyctaginaceae, which consists of flowering plants commonly known as the four o'clock family. This classification places Allionia arenaria within the order Caryophyllales, a group of flowering plants that share similar characteristics. The family Nyctaginaceae is part of the larger clade of eudicots, which includes the majority of flowering plant species. Allionia arenaria's classification also includes its position within the tribe Nyctagineae, a group of plants that share specific characteristics and growth habits. Further, it is also known by its synonym, Mirabilis arenaria, which is an alternative name used to refer to this species.
uses: Allionia arenaria is a plant species that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. Its roots and leaves are known to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have made it useful in treating various health conditions. intro: Allionia arenaria, also known as sandmat or winged sandmat, is a succulent plant native to the Americas. The health benefits of Allionia arenaria include its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels, as well as exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
